weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a clean, enamel-blue sky, the sun already bright and crisp at late morning. With no cloud to soften it, edges look sharpened and colors turn honest; step from shade to sun and you can feel the gentle winter lift. The air reads clear and dry, the kind that makes distant rooftops seem close, the kind that invites you to unhurriedly get on with the day.

The afternoon keeps the same serene tone: clear sky and steady light. UV will be moderate despite the season, so sunglasses and a little sunscreen are wise if you’re out around midday. It’s a pleasant day to move at a humane pace—grab a coffee at a quiet sidewalk table, run a couple of errands on foot, hang the washing, or take a short loop through the local park with a good song in your ear. Dress in light layers: long sleeves or a soft knit you can peel off in the sun, comfortable shoes, and a hat for the glare. In the sun it will feel companionable; in the shade, just brisk enough to keep you honest.
